sourceMovie.title: V for Vendetta
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: V for Vendetta (2006) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. James McTeigue's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Terminator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If V for Vendetta clicked for you, The Terminator (1984) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Matrix Revolutions
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If V for Vendetta clicked for you, The Matrix Revolutions (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after V for Vendetta?
faq.answer: Try The Terminator, The Matrix Revolutions, Insurgent—each shares DNA with V for Vendetta while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is V for Vendetta?
faq.answer: V for Vendetta (2006) is best described as Action, Thriller, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is V for Vendetta worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
